title: Pull Up in the Pocket
game_context: 5-on-5 half-court. You are the point guard initiating a high ball-screen at the top of the key. Your screener (5) sets a clean screen on your right. The screen defender (x5) is coached to drop on every ball-screen — he sits two steps below the level of the screen and protects the rim.
possession_setup: Top-of-the-key high pick-and-roll. The screen comes from the right. x5 is the read: he drops below the screen, chest below the level, weight back, feet pointing toward his own basket. The other three defenders are stationed in shell-rotation positions and do not stunt early.
decision_moment: You come off the screen with one dribble of separation. x5 has not stepped up. The pocket between the screen and x5 is open. Your on-ball defender (x1) is trailing on your hip, two steps behind.
visible_cue: x5's chest is below the level of the screen — clear drop posture. His feet point back toward the rim. The pocket between the ball-screen and x5 is wide open. Help defenders stay home.
prompt: You turn the corner off the screen. The big drops back. What's the read?

choices.label: Pull up in the pocket.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. Drop coverage gives you the pocket — rise into the open shot before x5 can recover.

choices.label: Snake the dribble back to the middle.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. Snaking buys time, but the pocket pull-up was already there. You let x1 catch up.
choices.partial_feedback_text: A snake works against late shows, not against a settled drop. Read the chest — drop = pull-up first.

choices.label: Drive at x5's chest into the paint.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Driving into a drop big walks you into the contest. The space is in front of him, not past him.

choices.label: Reset the ball to the wing.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. Resetting wastes the pocket. Drop coverage is asking you to take the pull-up.

best_read: Rise into the pull-up in the pocket. x5's posture is the green light — he's protecting the paint, not the screen, and there is no early help. The shot is open before the on-ball defender can recover.
acceptable_reads: Snake back to the middle if you misread the depth — keeps the possession alive but trades the open shot for a contested re-read.
bad_reads: Drive into x5's chest — walks into the protected paint and a contest at the rim.
Reset to the wing — wastes a clean drop-coverage advantage.
common_miss_reason: Reflex to attack downhill on every screen. A drop big is daring you to shoot; ball-handlers who never look at his chest never see the pocket.
why_best_read_works: Drop coverage is built around protecting the rim. The trade-off is the mid-range pocket — by definition the screen-defender cannot contest the pull-up without giving up his own assignment. The on-ball defender is trailing, so the only contest is x5 stepping up. He won't step up unless his coach changes the call.
decoder_teaching_point: When the screen defender drops, the pocket is yours. Read his chest before you turn the corner: chest below the screen + feet to his rim = pull up.

feedback.correct: Good read. The drop gave you the pocket — you took it before he could step up.
feedback.partial: Snaking works, but the drop already gave you the shot. Trust the chest cue.
feedback.wrong: Look at his chest. Below the screen + feet back = drop. The shot is in front of him, not past him.

self_review_checklist: Did I see the screen defender's chest before I committed?
Did I take the pocket pull-up, or hesitate?
Did I attack toward open space, not into a body?
Did I rise on balance off the dribble?

coach_validation.notes: Pack 2 (Phase β) — DROP-01 base scenario. Single-freeze, no secondBeat. D1 difficulty: undisguised drop, ball-handler reads coverage in the pocket. Approved for controlled production test under Pack 2 DROP foundations.

source_research_basis: Standard PnR coverage taxonomy. Drop coverage is the universal entry-level call at every level above middle school; the ball-handler's first read is the screen defender's depth, taught from Spain 'cinco' through every NBA staff manual.
explanation_md: **Read the Coverage — Drop.** When the screen defender drops, he's telling you the call: protect the paint, give up the pull-up. The cue is his chest — if it stays below the level of the screen and his feet point back at his rim, the pocket between the screen and his body is yours. The trade is honest: he won't contest the pull-up because that's not his job. The on-ball defender is behind you, the help is shell-honest, and the shot is up before x5 can step up. **Read his chest, then take the pocket.**

wrongDemos.caption: You snaked back. The big stayed home and x1 caught up.
You drove into x5's chest. The contest was already waiting.
You reset to the wing. The pocket closed before the ball moved again.
